evals.prompt: Break the candidate profiles design into issues. The living doc is at plans/candidate-profiles.md
evals.expected_output: Should produce ./issues/candidate-profiles/ with 5-7 numbered issue files and write ## Implementation Plan into the living doc. Each issue should have front-loaded context with file paths, line numbers, type signatures, and pattern references from the actual codebase.

evals.prompt: I want to convert plans/candidate-profiles.md into implementation issues, but it only has ## Requirements — no technical design yet.
evals.expected_output: Should detect the living doc has ## Requirements but no ## Technical Design and redirect the user to run /architect first. Should NOT generate issue files.

evals.prompt: ok the design is reviewed, let's break it down into issues. the living doc is at plans/candidate-profiles.md — I want to hand these off to agents
evals.expected_output: Same as eval 1 but with more casual phrasing. Should produce issue files with the same quality of context enrichment. The 'hand off to agents' framing should reinforce that issues need to be self-contained.

evals.prompt: Break plans/simple-fix.md into issues. It's a 1-phase design that just adds a validation check to two API endpoints.
evals.expected_output: Should handle a small/simple design gracefully — produce 1-2 issues, not force-split into more. Each issue should still have full codebase context (file paths, line numbers, pattern references). The ## Implementation Plan section should be proportionally brief. Should NOT pad with artificial issues to hit a minimum count.

evals.prompt: Break plans/platform-migration.md into issues. It has 8 phases with cross-cutting concerns across auth, billing, notifications, and the API layer.
evals.expected_output: Should produce 8-12 issues. Should correctly order dependencies (schema before API, API before UI, cross-cutting after the code they wire into). Should identify which issues can be parallelized. Should split any phase that touches 8+ files. New patterns (first cron job, first email template) should get their own focused issues even if small. Each issue should be self-contained for an agent context window.

evals.prompt: Break the design in plans/search.md into issues. The living doc has ## Rollback Notes from a previous failed decomposition attempt that said phases 3-5 were too broad.
evals.expected_output: Reads ## Rollback Notes for context about the previous failed attempt. Uses the rollback trigger info (phases 3-5 too broad) to inform decomposition — should split those phases more aggressively. Should NOT ignore the rollback notes or repeat the same decomposition that failed before. Should clear ## Rollback Notes after successful decomposition. If the design is still too broad even with aggressive splitting, should roll back to /architect again with new ## Rollback Notes specifying what needs further detail.
